latest.title: Exercise of Warrants
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 11:57
RNS Number : 6265W
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
13 March 2026
13 March 2026
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
("Borders & Southern" or the "Company")
Exercise of Warrants
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
(AIM: BOR), the
London
based independent oil and gas exploration company with appraisal and exploration assets offshore the
Falkland Islands, announces that it has received notification from Will Hodson, a Non-Executive Director, to exercise warrants over 105,263 new ordinary shares of
1 pence
each in the capital of the Company ("Warrant Shares") at the exercise price of
10 pence
per Warrant Share, for an aggregate cash value of
£10,526.30.
Application has been made for admission of the Warrant Shares to trading on AIM ("Admission") and it is expected that Admission will occur on or around 19 March 2026. The Warrant Shares will rank pari passu with the existing Ordinary Shares of 1 pence each in the capital of the Company.
Total Voting Rights
Following Admission, the total issued share capital of the Company will consist of 879,420,674 ordinary shares each with voting rights. The Company does not hold any ordinary shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company will be 879,420,674 and this is the figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For further information, please visit
www.bordersandsouthern.com
or contact:
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
Tel: 020 7071 6984

Notes to Editors:
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc is an oil & gas exploration company listed on the London Stock Exchange AIM (BOR). The Company operates and has a 100% interest in three Production Licences in the South Falkland Basin covering an area of nearly 10,000 square kilometres. The Company has acquired 2,517 square kilometres of 3D seismic and drilled two exploration wells, making a significant gas condensate discovery with its first well.
-ends-

latest.title: Sea Lion FID Announcement
latest.content:
10 Dec 2025 13:04
RNS Number : 0518L
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
10 December 2025
10 December 2025
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
("Borders & Southern" or the "Company")
Sea Lion FID Announcement
Borders & Southern (AIM: BOR), a London based independent oil and gas exploration company with appraisal and exploration assets offshore the Falkland Islands, notes the announcement by Rockhopper Exploration ("Rockhopper") this morning in which operator Navitas Petroleum and Rockhopper confirmed that a final investment decision ("FID") for the Sea Lion development project in the North Falkland Basin, offshore the Falkland Islands, has been taken.
Harry Baker, CEO of Borders and Southern, commented:
"We are delighted that Navitas and partner, Rockhopper, have taken FID on Sea Lion. It is a transformational moment for all stakeholders in the Falkland Islands. The significance of the region taking its first step towards being a fully-fledged oil and gas producing province cannot be overestimated. FID on Sea Lion proves that projects in the Falklands are bankable and further enhances the value of our project.
We believe that FID on Sea Lion as well as the opening up of a new oil province will re-focus attention from both the oil industry and its investors on the area and Darwin. Our world class Darwin prospect has over 460 million barrels of recoverable liquids, a phased development plan, very quick payback and huge upside with 40 plus prospects that could yield over 9 billion barrels of liquid hydrocarbons and 10Tcf of gas. We hold 100% of all our licenses. Borders & Southern look forward to updating the market as we continue to progress our Darwin development.
Borders & Southern are delighted for Sam Moody and his team at Rockhopper in bringing the Sea Lion project on this journey from concept to development."
For further information please visit
www.bordersandsouthern.com
or contact:
Borders & Southern Petroleum plc
via Tavistock
Harry Baker, CEO

company_30s.what_they_do: Borders Ltd is an oil‑and‑gas explorer that operates in the Falkland Islands, developing the Sea Lion field and other small offshore prospects.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The Falklands host untapped oil reserves that few other firms can reach. Borders’ focus on a single, high‑grade field gives it a niche advantage and the potential for a rapid turnaround if production starts.
company_30s.current_state: The company has just secured a Final Investment Decision for Sea Lion and is in the early development phase. It remains a small, thin‑liquidity play that trades at a fraction of the value of comparable assets.

rns_narrative: Insider buying and warrant exercise in early 2026 show that directors believe the company is undervalued and that new capital is on the way.
The December 2025 Sea Lion FID announcement confirms that the company has moved beyond the exploration stage into a definitive investment, a key milestone for any small oil play.
Quarterly and annual reports from 2025 illustrate that the company is reporting cash‑flow‑positive results and is on track to meet its production targets, signalling operational stability.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet hope amid a sea of indifference
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to insider confidence and the Sea Lion milestone as evidence that the market is under‑pricing the company’s upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the field may be too small to generate meaningful cash flow, that liquidity is thin, and that the company may struggle to raise further capital if production stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The narrative moved from cautious silence to a tentative optimism after the FID, but the overall chatter remains sparse.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, which means the market has not yet noticed the company—maximum surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: First production from Sea Lion in Q4 2026 would trigger a price breakout.
A positive cash‑flow forecast or a second field announcement would reinforce the bounce.
A spike in trading volume following the FID release could signal new institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 3‑week volume surge and a price break above the last 52‑week high; any announcement of a secondary offering or a partnership would also validate the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early post‑capitulation stage—APEX compression suggests a spring is ready to snap. A bounce is most likely once the company confirms that Sea Lion production is on track.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock sits at a 70‑plus percent drop from its all‑time high. If production starts and the price climbs to 50% of the ATH, the upside is roughly 100% from current levels. Historical deep‑crash signals have delivered 150‑200% returns in similar contexts.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If Sea Lion’s economics are weaker than expected or if the company fails to raise additional capital, the price could fall further into the 80‑plus percent drop range, wiping out the remaining upside.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 2‑3× current price versus a downside of 70% if the crash deepens. The asymmetry favors a long position with a tight stop.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day moving average, size the position to 10% of total capital, and set a stop 15% below entry. Use trailing stops once the price breaks above the recent high.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a 10‑day volume spike and a price breakout above the last 52‑week high. Also monitor any new partnership announcements or production data releases.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The company has not shown signs of imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deeply discounted Falkland oil explorer that just cleared a key development milestone—if Sea Lion starts producing, the upside is huge, but the risk of continued underperformance remains.
